item.title: Military unveils new $400 million Air Force One plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: The Air Force unveiled the Boeing 747-8 that will serve as the new Air Force One, providing the first look at the $400 million luxury plane.

item.title: Emergency services respond after two trains collide in England
item.description: There have been local media reports of injuries, but emergency services have not yet provided any details.

item.title: DOJ rebuffs judge's demand to state "anti-weaponization" fund is dead
item.description: A senior Justice Department official called a judge's demand for a declaration on the status of the "anti-weaponization" fund "unnecessary."

item.title: What to know as Israel and Hezbollah reach ceasefire; U.S.-Iran talks delayed
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah reached a ceasefire agreement Friday to end their fighting in Lebanon, three diplomats briefed on the matter told CBS News. Meanwhile, Talks between Iran and the U.S. expected to begin in Switzerland Friday were postponed, with the White House blaming logistical issues for the cancellation of JD Vance's trip to Geneva. CBS News' Robert Berger and Natalie Brand have more.

item.title: A father of 3 wanted to leave wealth to his kids. He turned to a coffee can.
item.description: Matthew Ankrum wanted to build wealth for his daughters and teach them about financial stability. A new book shares how.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire, officials say
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah have reached a Lebanon ceasefire agreement after their fighting delayed progression of the U.S.-Iran deal, a diplomat tells CBS News.

item.title: Expert warns on "bumpy road ahead" for Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire as U.S. seeks Iran deal
item.description: CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean is reacting to the latest Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire news after tensions rose in Lebanon, likely leading to U.S.-Iran talks being postponed.

item.title: Here's who qualifies for the Trump adminstration's student loan rate cut
item.description: The temporary discount applies to eligible federal Direct Loan borrowers who use automatic payments.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire now in effect after tensions flare up
item.description: An Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire is now in effect after tensions rose and impacted the U.S.-Iran talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 21)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Climber trapped on Everest for days says avalanche saved him
item.description: Dawa Sherpa was airlifted to Kathmandu, where doctors treated him for frostbite, severe dehydration and a fractured thigh bone.

item.title: Researchers find salty clouds surrounding "Pink Planet"
item.description: Researchers using the Webb Space Telescope discovered salty clouds surrounding a "Pink Plane." CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: What's open and closed for Juneteenth 2026?
item.description: Juneteenth will affect banking, mail service and financial markets, although retailers and restaurants are largely staying open.

item.title: Celebrating Juneteenth as Americans prepare to mark 250 years of U.S.
item.description: Americans are celebrating the Juneteenth holiday as the 250th anniversary of the U.S. approaches. Marc H. Morial, the president and CEO of the National Urban League, joins with more insight.

item.title: U.S. vs. Australia in FIFA World Cup match
item.description: The U.S. will play Australia in the FIFA World Cup on Friday. CBS News' Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Prediction markets are betting on celebrities to boost their brands
item.description: Kalshi has enlisted Lionel Messi and Timothée Chalamet as prediction markets compete to attract new users and cement their place in the mainstream.

item.title: How the Iran war united, and then divided Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: Trump has appeared during the Iran war to lose patience with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who may now find himself "stuck."

item.title: Italy nixes envoy's visit as leader Meloni "stunned" by Trump's remarks
item.description: Italian outlet says after a meeting at the G7 summit, Trump said Meloni had "begged me to take a picture with her," and he "felt sorry for her."

item.title: U.K. actress accused of trying to smuggle $208M of meth into Australia
item.description: Actress Emaa Hussen, who played alongside Jason Statham in "Redemption," is accused of trying to smuggle 700 pounds of methamphetamine into Australia.

item.title: "The Coffee Can Investor" offers a key strategy for families and long-term investors
item.description: "The Coffee Can Investor: A Stock-Picker's Journey to Build Generational Wealth" by former CBS News president Neeraj Khemlani, offers key advice for families and other long-term investors. CBS News' Jessica Moore has more.

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: New analysis shows what's missing from the Epstein files
item.description: A CBS News analysis of the released Epstein files shows what documents appear to be absent. CBS News' Vlad Duthiers has more on the search for answers surrounding the case against convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Scientists discover parasite that preys on insect-killing "zombie fungus"
item.description: Scientists say the "hyperparasite" preys on "zombie fungus" known to infect insects before subjecting them to a gruesome death.

item.title: Latest news on Iran deal as Israel and Hezbollah appear to reach ceasefire
item.description: Multiple diplomats tell CBS News that Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon after a tense period of fighting that could have jeopardized the U.S.-Iran agreement and subsequent process to reach a deal. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab reports, and Mark Cancian, a senior adviser for the Defense and Security Department at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins with more insight.

item.title: Questions swirl after 1-year-old boy fatally shot by police in Mississippi
item.description: A police shooting in Senatobia, Mississippi, that left a 1-year-old child dead has ignited simmering tensions between police and Black residents in the small town.

item.title: New book details the moment a GOP lawmaker appeared to lunge at Matt Gaetz
item.description: A new book recounts one of the most rambunctious moments on the floor of the House in modern history.

item.title: Researchers find "Pink Planet" has salty clouds: "We were very surprised"
item.description: The "Pink Planet," formally known as GJ504b, was discovered in 2013 and is technically not a planet but rather a "planetary-mass companion."

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Less than half of Americans can afford quality healthcare, Gallup finds
item.description: Gallup found that only 49% of Americans were "cost-secure" last year, with concerns about medical bills and prescription costs rising across income groups.

item.title: Extended interview: John Mulaney on his standup persona
item.description: In this web exclusive, standup comedian John Mulaney talks with correspondent Tracy Smith about his earliest experience learning about jokes; developing his on-stage persona (and why it involves a suit); why he's happiest writing for other people; and how he approaches his sobriety after having gone through rehab and becoming a father of two children.
